retrieved.title: Bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

retrieved.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
retrieved.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure

You need German language proficiency for all bachelor's and diploma programmes. The only exception is Mathematical Foundations of Data Science (because it is instructed in English).

- When you apply, you must provide a proof of German language proficiency at level A2.
- You decide for yourself whether your level of German is sufficient for the written test. Dictionaries are not allowed during the test.
- To be admitted to the degree programme, you need level C1. To reach this level, you can attend a German course as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU).

Find out everything you need to know about the recognised German language certificates and the VWU on this page.

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: Is proof of German language proficiency required?
retrieved.text:
All bachelor/diploma programmes as well as master programmes instructed in German require proof of [German language proficiency at level A2](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-a2/) at the time of application. Without such certification you cannot apply for admission.

To be admitted to the degree programme you need a proof of [German language proficiency at level C1](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-c1/). If you do not possess such a proof, the supplementary examination in German will be prescribed in your admission letter.

With this admission letter, you can be admitted as a non-degree student and attend a German course at the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) for up to four semesters.

retrieved.title: Legalisation and translation
heading_path: How do my documents become translated?
retrieved.text: We only accept documents that were issued in German or English. If your documents were issued in a different language, a translation is required. Please regard the following terms: - If full diplomatic certification is required, your documents have to be legalised first. Only then they may be translated including all stamps, seals, and certification notes. - An apostille must also be translated. This means that the apostille has to be affixed to the original document first. Afterwards it can be translated. - The documents must be translated by official sworn and court certified translators. - The translation must be inseparably attached to the original document. Do not detach the documents (not even when scanning them!). Else the translation will be void. # FAQ / - Which documents require diplomatic certification? We require diplomatic certification of all documents that attest your prior education. - Bachelor/diploma programmes: All pages of your secondary school leaving certificate and, if applicable, transcripts of records/diplomas (if you have studied at a university before). - Master programmes: Your higher education degree certificate, relevant transcripts of records and/or the diploma supplement. - My documents have not been diplomatically certified yet. Can I still apply for admission? No, as you may only apply once all documents have been diplomatically certified and translated, as required. If your application is incomplete, it may prolong its lead time. - For how

retrieved.title: Studying in the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
heading_path: Important note for students in the VWU
retrieved.text:
Once you have been admitted to the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities as a non-degree student, only the supplementary examination in German (Ergänzungsprüfung Deutsch, EPD)  is accepted as a proof of German language proficiency! It is not possible to leave the preparation programme and take a German language exam elsewhere. Disregard may lead to severe consequences such as the loss of your residence permit.

Persons who have quit the VWU and have passed a different German language exam in the meanwhile can still take the supplementary examination in German (EPD).

[Detailed information on the regulation](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/detailed-information/)

While you are admitted to the non-degree programme "attending the VWU" ("Besuch des Vorstudienlehrgangs"), you are only allowed to attend courses in the VWU and complete the prescribed supplementary examinations there. Taking exams from the degree programme is not allowed.

retrieved.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_path: We accept the following proofs of German
German language proficiency at level C1
retrieved.text:
- #### Proofs

  We only accept these proofs. There are no exceptions!

  - Secondary-school leaving certificate from a German-speaking school (with German as the language of instruction and examination\*) or an Austrian or German school abroad
  - Completion of a degree programme of at least three years with German as the language of instruction at a recognised post-secondary educational institution
  - Passing of the supplementary exam in German (Ergänzungsprüfung Deutsch) at a University Preparation Programme of an Austrian university

  \*Secondary-school leaving certificates from Austria, Germany, South Tyrol, Luxembourg and Liechtenstein are C1 certificates. You do not need to upload a proof of German with your application.

  The proofs listed here are valid indefinitely.
- #### Certificates

  We only accept these certificates. There are no exceptions!

  - Österreichisches Sprachdiplom – ÖSD Zertifikat C1 (formerly called: C1 Oberstufe Deutsch)
  - Goethe Institut – Goethe Zertifikat C1
  - telc Deutsch “C1 Hochschule”
  - Deutsche Sprachprüfung für den Hochschulzugang DSH2
  - Deutsches Sprachdiplom der Kultusministerkonferenz DSD II
  - Test Deutsch als Fremdsprache (Test DaF), at least level 4 in all parts
  - Language Center of the University of Vienna – course and successfully passed exam of level C1/2 or higher
  - Österreichischer Integrationsfonds (ÖIF) – ÖIF Test (C1) or higher

  The certificates may not be older than 3 years.

heading_path: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
- scan of your valid your passport or personal ID card (front and back!) as JPEG or PNG file
- all pages (!) of your original secondary-school leaving certificate
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- [list of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) in its original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - together with a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- if you have already studied: transcripts/diplomas in their original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- for the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science: [proof of English language proficiency](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/) at level B2 or higher

You have to submit the application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The application period depends on your citizenship. Please note: [degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) have a special application period.

retrieved.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
retrieved.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science-bachelor-with-aptitute-test/) | [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |

The language of instruction of these programmes is German and English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Economics](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/economics-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |
| [International Legal Studies](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/international-legal-studies-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) & [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |
| [Physics](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/physics-bachelor-with-osa/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |

reranked.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
reranked.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure

You need German language proficiency for all bachelor's and diploma programmes. The only exception is Mathematical Foundations of Data Science (because it is instructed in English).

- When you apply, you must provide a proof of German language proficiency at level A2.
- You decide for yourself whether your level of German is sufficient for the written test. Dictionaries are not allowed during the test.
- To be admitted to the degree programme, you need level C1. To reach this level, you can attend a German course as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU).

Find out everything you need to know about the recognised German language certificates and the VWU on this page.

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: Is proof of German language proficiency required?
reranked.text:
All bachelor/diploma programmes as well as master programmes instructed in German require proof of [German language proficiency at level A2](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-a2/) at the time of application. Without such certification you cannot apply for admission.

To be admitted to the degree programme you need a proof of [German language proficiency at level C1](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-c1/). If you do not possess such a proof, the supplementary examination in German will be prescribed in your admission letter.

With this admission letter, you can be admitted as a non-degree student and attend a German course at the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) for up to four semesters.

reranked.title: Legalisation and translation
heading_path: How do my documents become translated?
reranked.text: We only accept documents that were issued in German or English. If your documents were issued in a different language, a translation is required. Please regard the following terms: - If full diplomatic certification is required, your documents have to be legalised first. Only then they may be translated including all stamps, seals, and certification notes. - An apostille must also be translated. This means that the apostille has to be affixed to the original document first. Afterwards it can be translated. - The documents must be translated by official sworn and court certified translators. - The translation must be inseparably attached to the original document. Do not detach the documents (not even when scanning them!). Else the translation will be void. # FAQ / - Which documents require diplomatic certification? We require diplomatic certification of all documents that attest your prior education. - Bachelor/diploma programmes: All pages of your secondary school leaving certificate and, if applicable, transcripts of records/diplomas (if you have studied at a university before). - Master programmes: Your higher education degree certificate, relevant transcripts of records and/or the diploma supplement. - My documents have not been diplomatically certified yet. Can I still apply for admission? No, as you may only apply once all documents have been diplomatically certified and translated, as required. If your application is incomplete, it may prolong its lead time. - For how

reranked.title: Studying in the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
heading_path: Important note for students in the VWU
reranked.text:
Once you have been admitted to the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities as a non-degree student, only the supplementary examination in German (Ergänzungsprüfung Deutsch, EPD)  is accepted as a proof of German language proficiency! It is not possible to leave the preparation programme and take a German language exam elsewhere. Disregard may lead to severe consequences such as the loss of your residence permit.

Persons who have quit the VWU and have passed a different German language exam in the meanwhile can still take the supplementary examination in German (EPD).

[Detailed information on the regulation](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/detailed-information/)

While you are admitted to the non-degree programme "attending the VWU" ("Besuch des Vorstudienlehrgangs"), you are only allowed to attend courses in the VWU and complete the prescribed supplementary examinations there. Taking exams from the degree programme is not allowed.

reranked.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_path: We accept the following proofs of German
German language proficiency at level C1
reranked.text:
- #### Proofs

  We only accept these proofs. There are no exceptions!

  - Secondary-school leaving certificate from a German-speaking school (with German as the language of instruction and examination\*) or an Austrian or German school abroad
  - Completion of a degree programme of at least three years with German as the language of instruction at a recognised post-secondary educational institution
  - Passing of the supplementary exam in German (Ergänzungsprüfung Deutsch) at a University Preparation Programme of an Austrian university

  \*Secondary-school leaving certificates from Austria, Germany, South Tyrol, Luxembourg and Liechtenstein are C1 certificates. You do not need to upload a proof of German with your application.

  The proofs listed here are valid indefinitely.
- #### Certificates

  We only accept these certificates. There are no exceptions!

  - Österreichisches Sprachdiplom – ÖSD Zertifikat C1 (formerly called: C1 Oberstufe Deutsch)
  - Goethe Institut – Goethe Zertifikat C1
  - telc Deutsch “C1 Hochschule”
  - Deutsche Sprachprüfung für den Hochschulzugang DSH2
  - Deutsches Sprachdiplom der Kultusministerkonferenz DSD II
  - Test Deutsch als Fremdsprache (Test DaF), at least level 4 in all parts
  - Language Center of the University of Vienna – course and successfully passed exam of level C1/2 or higher
  - Österreichischer Integrationsfonds (ÖIF) – ÖIF Test (C1) or higher

  The certificates may not be older than 3 years.

reranked.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
reranked.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

reranked.title: Proof of German language proficiency
heading_path: General information about the German language proficiency
reranked.text: - You need a proof of German language proficiency at level A2 when applying for admission. - In order to acquire level C1, which is required for the admission to all bachelor/diploma programmes instructed in German and most master programmes, you may participate in a German course of the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) (VWU). - While attending the VWU, you have to [consider some things](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) regarding your admission to the University of Vienna. - In case you apply for a bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure, you will be able to participate in the procedure even though you do not yet have an adequate proof of German language proficiency. However, you must have this proof at the time of enrolment. - For the following master programmes, a proof of German language proficiency at level C1 or C2 is required for the application: - [Master programme in German as a Foreign and Second Language (C1)](/en/admission/master-programmes/german-as-a-foreign-and-second-language/) - [Master programme in Translation (C2)](https://transvienna.univie.ac.at/studium/masterstudium-translation/voraussetzungen/)It is not possible to attend a German course in the University Preparation Programme in order to be admitted to these master programmes. - In case you apply for a [master programme instructed in English](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/#c157833) or the [bachelor programme Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/entrance-exam/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/), you will not be able to attend a German course in the University Preparation Programme, as no proof of German language proficiency is required.

selected.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
selected.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure

You need German language proficiency for all bachelor's and diploma programmes. The only exception is Mathematical Foundations of Data Science (because it is instructed in English).

- When you apply, you must provide a proof of German language proficiency at level A2.
- You decide for yourself whether your level of German is sufficient for the written test. Dictionaries are not allowed during the test.
- To be admitted to the degree programme, you need level C1. To reach this level, you can attend a German course as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU).

Find out everything you need to know about the recognised German language certificates and the VWU on this page.

©
